zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Hulton於1329年出生在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯時，他的父親John de Hulton34歲，母親Alicia de Radcliffe29歲。他於1356年與Miss Arden de Harwarden在英國藍卡爾法爾沃斯結婚。他們至少是8個兒子的父母。他去世於1398年在他的家鄉享年69歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Hulton于1329年出生在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯时，他的父亲John de Hulton34岁，母亲Alicia de Radcliffe29岁。他于1356年与Miss Arden de Harwarden在英国藍卡爾法爾沃斯结婚。他们至少是8个儿子的父母。他去世于1398年在他的家乡享年69岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Hulton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire): habitational name from Hulton (Lancashire) or Hulton (Staffordshire), both from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Compare Hilton .

variants.name: Hilton
meanings: habitational name from any of various places called Hilton (e.g. in Cambridgeshire, Derbyshire, Staffordshire, North Yorkshire, and Westmorland), most of which derive from Old English hyll ‘hill’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’, though some come from Old English helde ‘slope’ + tūn.

occasionally, as in the case of Ralph filius Hilton (Yorkshire 1219), perhaps from the personal name Hilton, itself a possible form of Norman Hildun, composed of the ancient Germanic elements hild ‘strife, battle’ + hūn ‘bear cub’. Compare Hilt . This English surname is present in Ireland (mainly taken to Ulster in the early 17th century, though recorded earlier in Dublin).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

"The name Farnworth derives from the Anglo-Saxon word fearn or fern and weorthig, meaning farm or estate, and it therefore means ""fern-farm"". The village was established on higher ground 2 miles (3 km) to the north of the River Mersey and was for many years an isolated community. The earliest documentary evidence relating to the village is a charter dated 1352 when Henry, Duke of Lancaster established a halmote court for the manor of Widnes. The origins of the village are unknown. A chapel had been founded in the village about 1180 which was dedicated to St Wilfrid. At this time the village was part of the parish of Prescot. A grammar school was established in the village in 1507 by Bishop William Smyth who had been born in the village. Bishop Smyth also founded a chapel in the church for the use of his tenants in the village of Cuerdley and a footpath across the fields from Cuerdley to Farnworth; this was to allow the tenants to go to the church without passing along the main street of Farnworth when the plague was present."

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Hulton was born in 1329, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England, his father, John de Hulton, was 34 and his mother, Alicia de Radcliffe, was 29. He married Miss Arden de Harwarden in 1356, in Farnworth, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 8 sons. He died in 1398, in his hometown, at the age of 69.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Hulton est né en 1329, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, John de Hulton, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Alicia de Radcliffe, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Miss Arden de Harwarden en 1356, à Farnworth, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils. Il est décédé en 1398, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 69 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Hulton 1329 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John de Hulton 34 und seine Mutter Alicia de Radcliffe 29. Er hat 1356 in Farnworth, Lancashire, England Miss Arden de Harwarden geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne. Er starb 1398 im Alter von 69 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Hulton è nato il nel 1329 nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, John de Hulton, aveva 34 anni e il suo madre, Alicia de Radcliffe, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Miss Arden de Harwarden nel 1356, nel Farnworth, Lancashire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli. Morì nel 1398, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 69 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: William de Hultonが1329年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスで生まれた時，父John de Hultonは34歳，母Alicia de Radcliffeは29歳であった。1356年にイギリスランカシュアイアファルンウオルスでMiss Arden de Harwardenと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子の親であった。彼は1398年に故郷で69歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: William de Hulton(이)가 1329년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John de Hulton(은)는 34세였고, 그의 어머니 Alicia de Radcliffe(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1356년 영국 랭카실르 패른워스에서 Miss Arden de Harwarden(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1398년 그의 고향에서 69세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando William de Hulton nasceu em 1329, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John de Hulton, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Alicia de Radcliffe, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Miss Arden de Harwarden em 1356, em Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1398, em sua cidade natal, com 69 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William de Hulton родился в 1329 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени John de Hulton было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Alicia de Radcliffe, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Miss Arden de Harwarden в 1356 г. в местности фарнворт, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей. Он умер в 1398 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 69 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando William de Hulton nació en 1329, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra, su papá, John de Hulton, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Alicia de Radcliffe, tenía 29 años. Se casó con Miss Arden de Harwarden en 1356, en Farnworth, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos. Murió en 1398, en su pueblo, a la edad de 69 años.
